title: SSC CGL T-I Quant Section Test

questions.question: The cost of painting a rectangular sheet at the rate of ₹1.5 per square cm is ₹945. If the length of the rectangular sheet is 30 cm, then what is its breadth (in cm)?
questions.explanation: Given: Cost of painting = ₹945 Rate of painting = ₹1.5 per sq cm Length of sheet = 30 cm Concept Used: Area of rectangle = Length ×\times×​Breadth Cost of painting = Area ×\times×​Rate Solution: Area of sheet = Total costRate\frac{\text{Total cost}}{\text{Rate}}RateTotal cost​​ = 9451.5\frac{945 }{ 1.5}1.5945​​ = 630 sq cm Breadth = AreaLength\frac{\text{Area}}{\text{Length}}LengthArea​​ =63030 \frac{630 }{ 30 }30630​​= 21 cm Therefore, the breadth of the rectangular sheet is 21 cm. Option (b) is right.

questions.question: A and B can do a piece of work in 80 days; B and C can do it in 120 days; A and C can do it in 112 days. In how many days can B alone do it ?
questions.explanation: Given: A and B together can complete the work in 80 days. B and C together can complete the work in 120 days. A and C together can complete the work in 112 days. Formula Used: Total work = Efficiency ×\times× Time Solution: LCM (80, 129, 112) = 1680 = total work ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/64a6ef23-d0dd-4ede-9254-7fc7df59bb75.png) 2 (A + B + C) = 21 + 14 + 15 A + B + C = 25 Efficiency of B = Efficiency of A + B + C - Efficiency of A + C Efficiency of B = 25 - 15 = 10 TB = 168010\frac{1680}{10}101680​ TB = 168 days Thus, B can complete the work in 168 days. Thus, the correct option is (c) 168.

questions.question: 84 litre mixture of milk and water contains 30 percent milk. How much more milk must be added to make water percentage in the mixture as 60 percent?
questions.explanation: Given: 84L = 30% milk Solution: Milk : Water = 3 : 7 To get 4 : 6 Let, M = Milk to be added 84(0.3) + M = 0.4(84 + M) 0.6M = 8.4 M = 14 L Hence, the correct option is C.

questions.question: After 63 litres of petrol was poured into an empty storage tank, it was still 1% empty. How much petrol (in litres, rounded off to two decimal place) must be poured into the storage tank in order to fill it?
questions.explanation: Given: 63 litres makes the tank 99% full 1% of the tank is still empty Solution: Total Capacity = 63×10099=63.6363≈63.64 litres\frac{63 \times 100}{99} = 63.6363 \approx 63.64\ \text{litres}9963×100​=63.6363≈63.64 litres​​

questions.question: Which of the following has terminating decimal representation?
questions.explanation: Given: To determine if a fraction has a terminating decimal representation, we need to check its denominator when the fraction is in its simplest form. A fraction will have a terminating decimal representation if its denominator has only the prime factors 2 or 5. Concept Used: For a mixed fraction abca \frac{b}{c}acb​​we convert it to an improper fraction as follows: Improper Fraction = a+bc=(a⋅c)+bca + \frac{b}{c} = \frac{(a \cdot c) + b}{c} a+cb​=c(a⋅c)+b​​​ Once in improper fraction form, we simplify the denominator to check if it has only 2 and/or 5 as prime factors. Solution: Option A: 419=(4×9)+19=379 4 \frac{1}{9} = \frac{(4 \times 9) + 1}{9} = \frac{37}{9}491​=9(4×9)+1​=937​​ Option C: 2 13=(2×3)+13=73 \frac{1}{3} = \frac{(2 \times 3) + 1}{3} = \frac{7}{3}31​=3(2×3)+1​=37​​​ The denominator is 3, which is not 2 or 5, so this fraction does not have a terminating decimal representation. Option D: 115=(1×5)+15=651 \frac{1}{5} = \frac{(1 \times 5) + 1}{5} = \frac{6}{5}151​=5(1×5)+1​=56​​​ The denominator is 5, which is a prime factor of only 2 or 5. Therefore, this fraction has a terminating decimal representation. Thus, the correct answer is Option D: 115.1 \frac{1}{5}.151​.​​ ​

questions.question: Lalit and Manow started a business in partnership investing Rs.10,000 and Rs.18,000, respectively. After eight months, Nitin woined them by investing Rs. 24,000. What will be the total share of Nitin and Manow in the total profit of Rs. 22,000 earned at the end of 2 years from the starting of the business?
questions.explanation: Given: Lalit’s investment = Rs. 10,000 Manow’s investment = Rs. 18,000 Nitin’s investment = Rs. 24,000 Nitin woins after 8 months Total profit = Rs. 22,000 Duration of the business = 2 years Concept Used: Profit-sharing ratio in a partnership is based on the product of the amount of investment and the duration of the investment. Solution: Lalit’s share: Lalit’s investment = Rs. 10,000 for 2 years. Lalit’s share of the profit = 10,000 × 24 = 240,000 Manow’s share: Manow’s investment = Rs. 18,000 for 2 years. Manow’s share of the profit = 18,000 × 24 = 432,000 Nitin’s share: Nitin’s investment = Rs. 24,000, but he woined after 8 months. Nitin’s share of the profit = 24,000 × 16 = 384,000 The total share ratio is the sum of the products of each person's investment and time: Total share ratio = 240,000 : 432,000 : 384,000 = 5 : 9 : 8 = 22 unit 22 unit = 22,000 , 1 unit = 1000 Share of Nitin and Manow = ( 9 + 8) = 17 unit , = 17000 Thus, the total share of Nitin and Manow in the total profit of Rs. 22,000 is Rs. 17,000.

questions.question: A chord of length 24 cm is at a distance of 5 cm from the centre of a circle. the radius of the circle is ___________ cm.
questions.explanation: ​Given: Chord length = 24 cm. Distance from center to chord = 5 cm. Formula used: the perpendicular bisector theorem: r2=(Chord length2)2+Distance2r^2 = \left(\frac{\text{Chord length}}{2}\right)^2 + \text{Distance}^2r2=(2Chord length​)2+Distance2​​ Solution: By bisector theorem: r2=(242)2+52 r2=122+52 r2=144+25 r2=169 r=169 r=13 cmr^2 = \left(\frac{24}{2}\right)^2 + 5^2 \\ \ \\r^2 = 12^2 + 5^2 \\ \ \\r^2 = 144 + 25\\ \ \\ r^2 = 169 \\ \ \\ r = \sqrt{169} \\ \ \\ r = 13\ cmr2=(224​)2+52 r2=122+52 r2=144+25 r2=169 r=169​ r=13 cm​ ​

questions.question: The length of each edge of a cube is increased by 50%. The percentage increase in the surface area is:
questions.explanation: ​Given: Edge length increased by 50% Formula Used: Percentage increase in surface area=(New surface area−Original surface areaOriginal surface area)×100\text{Percentage increase in surface area} = \left(\frac{\text{New surface area} - \text{Original surface area}}{\text{Original surface area}}\right) \times 100Percentage increase in surface area=(Original surface areaNew surface area−Original surface area​)×100​​ Surface area of a cube: 6a2 Solution: Original surface area: 6a2 Increase length by 50% New edge length: 1.5a. New surface area: 6(1.5a)2=6×2.25a2=13.5a26(1.5a)^2 = 6 \times 2.25a^2 = 13.5a^26(1.5a)2=6×2.25a2=13.5a2 Increase in surface area: 13.5a2−6a2=7.5a213.5a^2 - 6a^2 = 7.5a^213.5a2−6a2=7.5a2​​ Percentage increase: (7.5a26a2)×100=125%\left(\frac{7.5a^2}{6a^2}\right) \times 100 = 125\%(6a27.5a2​)×100=125%

questions.question: A sum of money becomes double of itself in 7 years at a certain rate percent p.a. of compound interest, compounded annually. In how many years will it become eight times of itself at same rate of interest?
questions.explanation: Given: A sum of money doubles in 7 years at compound interest, compounded annually. We have to find the time it takes for the same sum to become eight times itself at the same rate of interest. Concept Used: A = P(1+R100)n P \left(1 + \frac{R}{100}\right)^nP(1+100R​)n​​ where: P is the principal, R is the rate of interest per annum, n is the time in years. Solution: Given that the sum doubles in 7 years, 2P = P(1+R100)7 P \left(1 + \frac{R}{100}\right)^7P(1+100R​)7​​ Divide both sides by P: 2 = (1+R100)7 \left(1 + \frac{R}{100}\right)^7(1+100R​)7..................(i) The sum to become 8 times itself: 8P = P(1+R100)nP \left(1 + \frac{R}{100}\right)^nP(1+100R​)n​​ 8 = (1+R100)n \left(1 + \frac{R}{100}\right)^n(1+100R​)n.....................(ii) Since: 8 = 232^323​​ From Equation (1): 23​ = ((1+R100)7)3=(1+R100)21\left(\left(1 + \frac{R}{100}\right)^7\right)^3 = \left(1 + \frac{R}{100}\right)^{21}((1+100R​)7)3=(1+100R​)21​​ Comparing with Equation (2): ​(1+R100)n=(1+R100)21\left(1 + \frac{R}{100}\right)^n = \left(1 + \frac{R}{100}\right)^{21}(1+100R​)n=(1+100R​)21​​ Thus: n = 21 years Thus, the correct option is (b) 21 years Alternate method: 21 → 7 years 23 → 7 ×3\times 3×3 =21 years ​

questions.question: If tanA + cotA = 2 and A ∈ (0, π/2), then what is sinA + cosA?
questions.explanation: Given: tan A + cot A = 2, where A ∈ (0, π/2) Formula Used: sin²A + cos²A = 1 Solution: tan⁡A+cot⁡A=sin⁡Acos⁡A+cos⁡Asin⁡A =sin⁡2A+cos⁡2Asin⁡Acos⁡A tan⁡A+cot⁡A=2 =&gt1sin⁡Acos⁡A=2 =&gtsin⁡Acos⁡A=12 (sin⁡A+cos⁡A)2 =sin⁡2A+cos⁡2A+2sin⁡Acos⁡A =1+2×12 =2 =&gtsin⁡A+cos⁡A=2 \tan A + \cot A = \frac{\sin A}{\cos A} + \frac{\cos A}{\sin A} \\\ \\= \frac{\sin^2 A + \cos^2 A}{\sin A \cos A}\\\ \\\tan A + \cot A = 2 \\\ \\\Rightarrow \frac{1}{\sin A \cos A} = 2\\\ \\\Rightarrow \sin A \cos A = \frac{1}{2}\\\ \\(\sin A + \cos A)^2 \\\ \\= \sin^2 A + \cos^2 A + 2\sin A \cos A\\\ \\= 1 + 2 \times \frac{1}{2}\\\ \\= 2\\\ \\\Rightarrow \sin A + \cos A = \sqrt{2}\\\ \\tanA+cotA=cosAsinA​+sinAcosA​ =sinAcosAsin2A+cos2A​ tanA+cotA=2 =&gtsinAcosA1​=2 =&gtsinAcosA=21​ (sinA+cosA)2 =sin2A+cos2A+2sinAcosA =1+2×21​ =2 =&gtsinA+cosA=2​ ​​

questions.question: AB is the chord of the circle with centre O. A line segment DOC originating from a point D on the circumference of the circle in mawor segment meets AB produced at C such that BC = OD. If angle BCO = 30°, then angle AOD is:
questions.explanation: Given: In the circle: OD = BC = OB (radii of the circle). ∠BCO = 30° (given). We need to find ∠AOD. Concept used: In an isosceles triangle, angles opposite equal sides are equal. The sum of angles in a triangle = 180°. Linear pair property: Angles on a straight line sum to 180°. Solution: ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/f1f29312-fbcd-48f9-8fac-ee76b6e97555.png) In ΔBOC Since OB = BC, ΔBOC is isosceles: ∠BCO = ∠BOC = 30°. Find ∠OBC in ΔBOC The sum of angles in ΔBOC = 180°: ∠OBC = 180° − (∠BCO + ∠BOC) ∠OBC = 180° − 30° − 30° ∠OBC = 120°. Find ∠OBA in ΔOBA Since OB = OA (radii of the circle), ΔOBA is isosceles: ∠OBA = ∠OAB. Using the linear pair property: ∠OBA = 180° − ∠OBC ∠OBA = 180° − 120° ∠OBA = 60°. Find ∠AOB in ΔOBA The sum of angles in ΔOBA = 180°: ∠AOB = 180° − (∠OAB + ∠OBA) ∠AOB = 180° − 60° − 60° ∠AOB = 60°. Find ∠AOD Apply linear pair property in ∠AOD, ∠AOB and ∠BOC: ∠AOD = 180° − (∠AOB + ∠BOC) ∠AOD = 180° − 60° − 30° ∠AOD = 90°. ∴ The value of ∠AOD is 90°.

questions.question: If sinA = 3/5 and cosB = 12/13, find sinAcosB + cosAsinB :
questions.explanation: ​Given:sin⁡A=35, cos⁡B=1213Formula Used:sin⁡(A+B)=sin⁡Acos⁡B+cos⁡Asin⁡BSolution:cos⁡A=45, sin⁡B=513sin⁡Acos⁡B+cos⁡Asin⁡B=35×1213+45×513=36+2065=5665Final Answer:5665\textbf{Given:}\\\sin A=\frac{3}{5},\ \cos B=\frac{12}{13}\\[6pt]\textbf{Formula Used:}\\\sin(A+B)=\sin A\cos B+\cos A\sin B\\[6pt]\textbf{Solution:}\\\cos A=\frac{4}{5},\ \sin B=\frac{5}{13}\\\sin A\cos B+\cos A\sin B\\=\frac{3}{5}\times\frac{12}{13}+\frac{4}{5}\times\frac{5}{13}\\=\frac{36+20}{65}=\frac{56}{65}\\[6pt]\textbf{Final Answer:}\\\frac{56}{65}Given:sinA=53​, cosB=1312​Formula Used:sin(A+B)=sinAcosB+cosAsinBSolution:cosA=54​, sinB=135​sinAcosB+cosAsinB=53​×1312​+54​×135​=6536+20​=6556​Final Answer:6556​​​

questions.question: 8 men can do a piece of work in 8 days while 16 women can do a piece of work in 5 days. In how many days 4 men and 5 women together can do this work?
questions.explanation: Given: 8 men complete work in 8 days, 16 women complete work in 5 days. Solution: Total work = 8 × 8 = 64 man-days Total work = 16 × 5 = 80 woman-days So, 64 man-days = 80 woman-days 1 man = 1.25 women 4 men = 5 women So 4 men + 5 women = 10 women Work per day = 10 women Time required = 80 ÷ 10 = 8 days Final Answer: So the correct answer is (c)

questions.question: ₹37,600 were divided among A, B and C such that 4 times of A = 6 times of B = 7 times of C. Find the share of A.
questions.explanation: ​Given:Total amount = 376004A = 6B = 7CConcept Used:Ratio and proportionFormula Used:If k1A=k2B=k3C, then A:B:C=1k1:1k2:1k3Solution:4A=6B=7CA:B:C=14:16:17LCM of 4,6,7 = 84A:B:C=21:14:12Sum of ratio parts = 21+14+12 = 47Share of A = 2147×37600= 16800Final Answer:16800\textbf{Given:} \\[6pt]\text{Total amount} \ = \ 37600 \\[6pt]4A \ = \ 6B \ = \ 7C \\[10pt]\textbf{Concept Used:} \\[6pt]\text{Ratio and proportion} \\[10pt]\textbf{Formula Used:} \\[6pt]\text{If } k_1A = k_2B = k_3C, \text{ then } A : B : C = \frac{1}{k_1} : \frac{1}{k_2} : \frac{1}{k_3} \\[10pt]\textbf{Solution:} \\[6pt]4A = 6B = 7C \\[6pt]A : B : C = \frac{1}{4} : \frac{1}{6} : \frac{1}{7} \\[10pt]\text{LCM of } 4,6,7 \ = \ 84 \\[6pt]A : B : C = 21 : 14 : 12 \\[10pt]\text{Sum of ratio parts} \ = \ 21 + 14 + 12 \ = \ 47 \\[10pt]\text{Share of } A \ = \ \frac{21}{47} \times 37600 \\[6pt]= \ 16800 \\[10pt]\textbf{Final Answer:} \\[6pt]16800Given:Total amount = 376004A = 6B = 7CConcept Used:Ratio and proportionFormula Used:If k1​A=k2​B=k3​C, then A:B:C=k1​1​:k2​1​:k3​1​Solution:4A=6B=7CA:B:C=41​:61​:71​LCM of 4,6,7 = 84A:B:C=21:14:12Sum of ratio parts = 21+14+12 = 47Share of A = 4721​×37600= 16800Final Answer:16800​​

questions.question: Ravi and Mohan start moving together from a point in opposite directions on bikes. Ravi’s speed is 80 km/h and Mohan’s speed is 40 km/h. What will be the distance between them after 15 min?
questions.explanation: Given: Ravi’s speed is 80 km/h and Mohan’s speed is 40 km/h. Formula Used: Distance = Speed ×\times×​ Time Solution: The time is given as 15 minutes, which is equivalent to: 1560\frac{15}{60}6015​​ = 0.25 hours. The distance covered by Ravi in 15 minutes is: 80× \times×​ 0.25 = 20 km The distance covered by Mohan in 15 minutes is: 40 ×\times×​ 0.25 = 10 km The total distance between them is: 20 + 10 = 30 km Thus, the distance between Ravi and Mohan after 15 minutes is 30 km.

questions.question: The radius of a circle is increased by 25 percent. What will be the percentage increase in its area?
questions.explanation: Given: - Radius of a circle is increased by 25% Formula Used: - Area of a circle = π × r² - If radius is increased by x%, then: Percentage Increase in Area = [(1+x100)2–1]×100[(1 + \frac{x}{100})² – 1] × 100[(1+100x​)2–1]×100​​ Solution: Let the original radius = r New radius = r + 25% of r = 1.25r Original area = π × r² New area = π × (1.25r)² = π × 1.5625r² Increase in area = New Area – Original Area = π × 1.5625r² – π × r² = π × r² × (1.5625 – 1) = π × r² × 0.5625 Percentage Increase = (IncreaseOriginal)×100(\frac{Increase }{ Original}) × 100(OriginalIncrease​)×100​​ = (π×r2×0.5625π×r2)×100(\frac{π × r² × 0.5625 }{π × r²}) × 100(π×r2π×r2×0.5625​)×100​​ = 0.5625 × 100 = 56.25%

questions.question: The total surface area of a right pyramid, with base as a square of side 6cm, is 312 cm². What is the slant height of pyramid (nearest perfect number in cm) ?
questions.explanation: Given: Base = square of side 6 cm Total surface area = 312 cm² Lateral faces are 4 congruent triangles Formula Used: Total surface area = base area + lateral area Lateral area = 4 × (½ × base × slant height) = 2 × base × slant height Solution: Let the slant height of the pyramid be l Base area = (6)2 = 36 cm² Now, from the formula of TSA of pyramid; 312 = 36 + 2 × 6 × l 312 - 36 = 12l 276 = 12l l = 27612\frac{276}{12}12276​​ = 23cm

questions.question: In a triangle ABC, AB = AC. BC is extended to D such that CD = AB and the angle ADC is 30°. What are the angles of triangle ABC?
questions.explanation: Given: In triangle ABC, AB = AC. BC is extended to D such that CD = AB and ∠ADC = 30°. Formula Used: In an isosceles triangle, the base angles are equal. The sum of angles in any triangle = 180°. Solution: ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/292b9845-2d63-472c-a45b-95a1e0f80c91.png) ​Since AB = AC, triangle ABC is isosceles, and ∠B = ∠C. In triangle ADC, we know CD = AB, and ∠ADC = 30°. Therefore, triangle ADC is also isosceles with ∠DAC = ∠DCA. Since ∠ADC = 30°, the sum of the other two angles in triangle ADC is 180° - 30° = 150°. => ∠DAC = ∠DCA = 150° ÷ 2 = 75°. Now, in triangle ABC: ∠B + ∠C + ∠A = 180°. Since ∠B = ∠C and ∠B + ∠C = 180° - ∠A, and ∠A = 60° (as given), => ∠B = ∠C = 60°. ∴ The angles of triangle ABC are 60°, 60°, and 60°.

questions.question: What fraction of a day is 14 hours ?
questions.explanation: ​Concept Used:1 day=24 hoursSolution:Total hours in a day=24 hoursFraction of a day to 14 hours=1424=712So, fraction of a day is 712.Hence, the correct answer is 712.\textbf{Concept Used:} \\[4pt]1 \text{ day} = 24 \text{ hours} \\[8pt]\textbf{Solution:} \\[4pt]\text{Total hours in a day} = 24 \text{ hours} \\[6pt]\text{Fraction of a day to } 14 \text{ hours} = \frac{14}{24} = \frac{7}{12} \\[8pt]\text{So, fraction of a day is } \frac{7}{12}. \\[10pt]\textbf{Hence, the correct answer is } \boxed{\frac{7}{12}}.Concept Used:1 day=24 hoursSolution:Total hours in a day=24 hoursFraction of a day to 14 hours=2414​=127​So, fraction of a day is 127​.Hence, the correct answer is 127​​.​​

questions.question: The ratio in which point (4, 5) divides the line woining two points (2, 3) and (7, 8) is:
questions.explanation: SOLUTION: The co-ordinates of a point which divided two points (x1,y1x_1, y_1x1​,y1​​) and (x2,y2x_2, y_2x2​,y2​​) internally in the ratio m:n is given by the formula, ​(x,y)=[(mx2+nx1m+n),(my2+ny1m+n)](x, y) = \left[\left(\frac{ mx_2+nx_1}{m+n}\right), \left(\frac{ my_2+ny_1}{m+n}\right)\right](x,y)=[(m+nmx2​+nx1​​),(m+nmy2​+ny1​​)]​​ Here it is said that the point (4, 5) divides the points A(2,3) and B(7,8). Substituting these values in the above formula we have, ​(5,y)=[(m(7)+n(2)m+n),(m(8)+n(3)m+n)](5, y) = \left[\left(\frac{ m(7)+n(2)}{m+n}\right), \left(\frac{ m(8)+n(3)}{m+n}\right)\right](5,y)=[(m+nm(7)+n(2)​),(m+nm(8)+n(3)​)]​​ Equating the individual components we have, ​4=m(7)+n(2)m+n 4m+4n=7m+2n 3m=2n mn=234 = \frac{m(7) + n(2)}{m + n} \\ \ \\4m + 4n = 7m + 2n \\ \ \\3m = 2n \\ \ \\\frac{m}{n} = \frac{2}{3}4=m+nm(7)+n(2)​ 4m+4n=7m+2n 3m=2n nm​=32​​​

questions.question: If x−1x=5x - \frac1x = 5x−x1​=5​, then the value of x4+1x4x^4 + \frac1{x^4}x4+x41​​ is:

The average salary of all the workers in a factory is ₹14,000. The average salary of 80 skilled workers is ₹15,000 and the average salary of the remaining workers is ₹12,000. Find the total number of workers in the factory.
questions.explanation: ​Given:Average salary of all workers=14000Skilled workers=80Average salary of skilled workers=15000Average salary of remaining workers=12000Formula Used:Total Salary=Average×Number of WorkersSolution:Let total workers=x14000x=15000×80+12000(x−80)14000x=1200000+12000x−96000014000x=12000x+2400002000x=240000x=120\textbf{Given:} \\\text{Average salary of all workers} = 14000 \\\text{Skilled workers} = 80 \\\text{Average salary of skilled workers} = 15000 \\\text{Average salary of remaining workers} = 12000 \\\textbf{Formula Used:} \\\text{Total Salary} = \text{Average} \times \text{Number of Workers} \\\textbf{Solution:} \\\text{Let total workers} = x \\14000x = 15000 \times 80 + 12000(x - 80) \\14000x = 1200000 + 12000x - 960000 \\14000x = 12000x + 240000 \\2000x = 240000 \\x = 120 \\Given:Average salary of all workers=14000Skilled workers=80Average salary of skilled workers=15000Average salary of remaining workers=12000Formula Used:Total Salary=Average×Number of WorkersSolution:Let total workers=x14000x=15000×80+12000(x−80)14000x=1200000+12000x−96000014000x=12000x+2400002000x=240000x=120​​

questions.question: Let ABC and DEF be two triangles such that ∠A = ∠D = 40° and AB = DE. What is the condition required to be met such that ABC and DEF are similar triangles?
questions.explanation: Given∠A=∠D=40∘AB=DEFormula UsedIf two angles of one triangle are equal to two angles of another, the triangles are similarSolution∠A=∠DIf ∠C=∠FThen two corresponding angles are equal△ABC∼△DEFFinal Answer∠C=∠F\textbf{Given}\\\angle A = \angle D = 40^\circ\\AB = DE\\\textbf{Formula Used}\\\text{If two angles of one triangle are equal to two angles of another, the triangles are similar}\\\textbf{Solution}\\\angle A = \angle D\\\text{If } \angle C = \angle F\\\text{Then two corresponding angles are equal}\\\triangle ABC \sim \triangle DEF\\\textbf{Final Answer}\\\boxed{\angle C = \angle F}Given∠A=∠D=40∘AB=DEFormula UsedIf two angles of one triangle are equal to two angles of another, the triangles are similarSolution∠A=∠DIf ∠C=∠FThen two corresponding angles are equal△ABC∼△DEFFinal Answer∠C=∠F​​​

questions.question: A shopkeeper bought 5 identical chairs for a total cost of Rs. 7,500. He sold three chairs at a gain of 20% each and the remaining two chairs at a loss of 10% each. What is his overall profit or loss percentage?
questions.explanation: ​GivenTotal chairs=5Total Cost Price=7500Gain on 3 chairs=20%Loss on 2 chairs=10%Formula UsedProfit %=Total SP−Total CPTotal CP×100SolutionCost price per chair=75005=1500SP of one chair at 20% gain=1500×120100=1800SP of 3 chairs=3×1800=5400SP of one chair at 10% loss=1500×90100=1350SP of 2 chairs=2×1350=2700Total SP=5400+2700=8100Total CP=7500Profit=8100−7500=600Profit %=6007500×100=8%Final AnswerOverall profit=8%\textbf{Given} \\\text{Total chairs} = 5 \\\text{Total Cost Price} = 7500 \\\text{Gain on 3 chairs} = 20\% \\\text{Loss on 2 chairs} = 10\% \\\textbf{Formula Used} \\\text{Profit \%} = \frac{\text{Total SP} - \text{Total CP}}{\text{Total CP}} \times 100 \\\textbf{Solution} \\\text{Cost price per chair} = \frac{7500}{5} = 1500 \\\text{SP of one chair at } 20\% \text{ gain} = 1500 \times \frac{120}{100} = 1800 \\\text{SP of 3 chairs} = 3 \times 1800 = 5400 \\\text{SP of one chair at } 10\% \text{ loss} = 1500 \times \frac{90}{100} = 1350 \\\text{SP of 2 chairs} = 2 \times 1350 = 2700 \\\text{Total SP} = 5400 + 2700 = 8100 \\\text{Total CP} = 7500 \\\text{Profit} = 8100 - 7500 = 600 \\\text{Profit \%} = \frac{600}{7500} \times 100 = 8\% \\\textbf{Final Answer} \\\text{Overall profit} = 8\%GivenTotal chairs=5Total Cost Price=7500Gain on 3 chairs=20%Loss on 2 chairs=10%Formula UsedProfit %=Total CPTotal SP−Total CP​×100SolutionCost price per chair=57500​=1500SP of one chair at 20% gain=1500×100120​=1800SP of 3 chairs=3×1800=5400SP of one chair at 10% loss=1500×10090​=1350SP of 2 chairs=2×1350=2700Total SP=5400+2700=8100Total CP=7500Profit=8100−7500=600Profit %=7500600​×100=8%Final AnswerOverall profit=8%​​

questions.question: The difference between a number increased by 17% and the same number decreased by 18% is 28. Find the number.
questions.explanation: Given: Difference between 17% increase and 18% decrease of a number = 28 Solution: Let the number be x Increased value = x + 0.17x = 1.17x Decreased value = x - 0.18x = 0.82x Difference = 1.17x - 0.82x = 0.35x Now, 0.35x = 28 x = 280.35\frac{28}{0.35}0.3528​​ = 80
